paper_title: Characterization of single nucleotide polymorphisms in chloroplast genomes of Musaceae Juss

entities.description: The study of the genomes of plants, specifically the chloroplast genomes of the Musaceae family.
The study of evolutionary relationships among biological entities, often using genetic data.
Author of the research article.
Corresponding author of the research article.
Affiliation of the authors.
University where the NTT Hi-Tech Institute is located.
Variation in a single nucleotide that occurs at a specific position in the genome.
The autonomous genome found within chloroplasts, also known as the plastome.
A measure of the degree of polymorphism within a population.
Conservative regions in the plastid genome whose structure can expand or contract.
Software used for sequence alignment, SNP detection, and genome structure analysis.
Algorithm used to identify structural changes in the chloroplast genome.
Software used for nucleotide diversity analysis and Pi values calculation.
Algorithm used for aligning whole chloroplast genome sequences.
Software used to calculate Bayesian Inference (BI) values for phylogenetic analysis.
Software used to identify the optimal phylogenetic model.
Source of 55 published Musaceae complete chloroplast genome sequences.
Public database of the banana genome.
The proportion of adenine and thymine bases in the Musaceae plastid genomes.
The measured variation in size of the Inverted Repeat regions in Musaceae.
Bayesian Inference value indicating 100% accuracy in the phylogenetic branches.
Threshold used to determine the stability of the evolutionary model.
